purpose: Documentation page for the architecture area: Synapse Architecture Overview. It explains operational behavior, user workflow, data ownership, or architecture details that future agents use when updating Synapse docs or app behavior. Key content: Synapse is a standalone distributed control system for coordinating autonomous agent swarms. It decomposes complex software development tasks into parallel work streams, dispatches them to independent worker agents, and provides a real-time dashboard for monitoring progress. Synapse is project-agnostic and operates independently from the projects it manages. The file is 299 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
gotchas: Documentation may describe behavior that has moved into Electron IPC/services or .claude skills; verify implementation before making broad user-facing claims.
Stale dependency reference: this file says React 18, but package.json currently uses react/react-dom ^19.2.4; documentation updates should say React 19 unless discussing history.
patterns: Documentation pattern: H1 title, short introduction, then operational sections with headings, tables, examples, or path references for future maintainers.
Uses fenced code blocks for command examples, schemas, or prompt templates.
Uses markdown tables for compact command, file, or option matrices.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
ASCII double hyphen is used in prose where many docs avoid Unicode punctuation for terminal readability.
